cards.title: Word 'Spinster'
cards.body: The word 'spinster' originally meant a person who spun yarn, and only later came to refer to an unmarried woman.

cards.title: Linguistic 'Lunch'
cards.body: The word 'lunch' originated in the 16th century, when it referred to a small snack eaten between meals, and is likely derived from the Old English word 'nuncheon', meaning 'a small piece of food'.

cards.title: History of 'Hazard'
cards.body: The word 'hazard' originated in the 14th century, and is derived from the Arabic word 'al-zahr', meaning 'the die', as it was initially used to refer to a dice game.

cards.title: Origin of 'Thug'
cards.body: The word 'thug' originated in India in the 13th century, and was initially used to refer to a member of a gang of professional robbers and murderers known as the 'Thuggee'.

cards.title: Fascinating 'Flop'
cards.body: The word 'flop' originated in the 16th century, and initially referred to the sound of something falling flat, such as a bird's wings.

cards.title: Story of 'Sob'
cards.body: The word 'sob' originated in the 14th century, and is believed to be derived from the Old English word 'sobban', which meant 'to sigh or lament'.

cards.title: History of 'Toast'
cards.body: The word 'toast' originated in the 14th century, and was initially used to refer to a piece of bread that had been browned or crisped, often served as a accompaniment to drinks.

cards.title: Etymology of 'Panic'
cards.body: The word 'panic' originated in ancient Greece, and is derived from the name of the god Pan, who was believed to cause fear and confusion in those who encountered him.

cards.title: Linguistic 'Chat'
cards.body: The word 'chat' originated in the 15th century, and is derived from the Old French word 'chater', meaning 'to converse or talk'.
